The table aggregates insights related to auction performance for hotel HOPS partners. It includes data on impressions, clicks, bids tied to HOPS partners nd campaigns. Potential use cases include analyzing the effectiveness of various campaigns, understanding market trends, and evaluating partner performance over time.

This table contains aggregated data on hotel booking campaign results, combining information from partner websites and Skyscanner's hotel listings. It captures daily metrics such as booking details (length of stay, guest count, booking window), partner campaign and hotel identifiers, pricing averages, as well as performance indicators like impressions, clicks, and ad ranking. Use cases include analyzing campaign effectiveness, comparing pricing competitiveness, and understanding user engagement across different partners and hotels.

Offer-level hotel search results with one row per (dt, correlation_id, location_entity_id, website_id). Contains pricing, ranking, and competitive position data for each partner offer served in hotel search auctions. Source: hotel_result_event.

Grain: one row per date x website_id x campaign_name x partner_hotel_id x location_entity_id x traveller_preferred_country_code x platform_device x length_of_stay_days x booking_window_days x guests. Auction participation metrics for events having bidding service enriching the quotes.

Grain: one row per partner (partner_id), traveller preferred country code, and property country location per day (dt). Measures click-weighted coverage to assess whether a partner is mapped to the right Skyscanner inventory.

Grain: one row per date × website_id × campaign_name × partner_hotel_id × location_entity_id × traveller_preferred_country_code × platform_device × length_of_stay_days × booking_window_days × guests x display rank. Auction results metrics for all hotel partners. Aggregation of different auction results.
Grain: one row per redirect event (redirect_id). Redirect-level partner performance with attributed booking and revenue metrics.
Grain: one row per dt × website_id × traveller_preferred_country_code × platform_device. Daily share of voice metrics for hotel commercial partners.

Grain: one row per dt x website_id x location_entity_id x partner_hotel_id x traveller_preferred_country_code x event_source_type x commercial_deal_type x os_name (and additional segment dimensions). Pre-aggregated daily hotel redirect and booking summary by partner and property.

Grain: one row per dt × redirect_type × website_id × traveller_preferred_country_code × platform_device × booking_window_days × accommodation_type × stay_country_code × is_cheapest_price × redirect_source. Daily price accuracy analysis per partner and segment.
Grain: one row per dt × website_id. Daily hotel property mapping and release metrics per partner.

This table contains detailed performance metrics related to hotel partner redirects, including booking and revenue data. It records information on user interactions with hotel partner offers, capturing details like booking dates, stay duration, guest counts, device types, marketing campaign attributes, and traffic sources. This data can be used to analyze partner performance, track marketing campaign effectiveness, understand user booking behavior, and assess revenue contributions from different traffic and device segments. It is useful for evaluating commercial deals with hotel partners and optimizing marketing efforts.

After a user performs a search for flights, hotels, or car rentals, they are presented with options that match their search criteria. 
If they choose a specific option, they are taken to the Booking Panel, which shows quotes from different suppliers. 
If they then select a quote, they will be redirected to a partner website (or the Skyscanner booking platform, if booking through Skyscanner) to continue with the booking process. 
This type of event is known as a redirect or click. 
The gold flight redirects dataset represents all redirects that occur across flights verticals and includes - 
 1. Core information that is available for every redirect. Example - redirect_id, event_id etc. 
 2. Dataset has been enriched with DeviceAtlas fields. Example - os_name etc. 
 3. Estimated revenue generated per redirect. 
 4. Various dimension keys for grouping redirect revenue.

**Granularity**: One row per redirect(redirect id per dt)

**Data contract**: [Gold+-+f+flight+redirect](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DATASET/pages/1047720737/Gold+-+Fact+-+f+flight+redirect)

**Data pipeline**: [gold_redirects_dag_v1](https://cloud.astronomer.io/clvceu04b012p01my370eiwjx/dags/clvcfhr4301bs01nbz46zwutk/gold_redirects_dag_v1)

**Data pipeline (Github)**: [gold-redirects](https://github.com/Skyscanner/gold-redirects)

Latest state of Placements IO line items (joined with related campaign, opportunity, and account) as-of dt, scoped to line items active on dt (line_item_start_date <= dt <= line_item_end_date). On the latest run only, the window is widened to also include line items booked up to 6 months in the future (line_item_end_date >= dt AND line_item_start_date <= dt + 6 months). Backfills keep the active-on-dt window. Powers downstream billing tables such as booked revenue.

**Granularity**: One row per line item active on dt (plus up to +6 months of future-booked line items on the latest run)

**Data Pipeline**: [ads_placementsio_dag](https://skyscanner.astronomer.run/d46zwutk/dags/ads_placementsio_dag/grid)

Dataset capturing delivery metrics of advertising line items. It only covers in-house ads, i.e., those managed through Skyscanner Ads Manager (SAM). The metrics are reported per line item in a given (i) market, (ii) route (defined by its origin and destination) and (iii) placement - these three properties are collectively referred to as targeting. The dataset aims to provide readily available insights into (i) the competition a line item is facing, (ii) its budget-increasing potential, (iii) reasons for underperformance (if applicable). See column descriptions for more detailed info.

**Granularity**: One row per line item, market, route (i.e., origin and destination) and placement

**Data contract**: [Line Item Competition Data Contract](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/TsKQP)

Hotel vendor price accuracy, we are comparing redirect prices we show on the booking panel and the ones that partners show on their website. We use price and currency info in the redirect event, hotels partners events to compare Skyscanner redirect price vs partner payment price & partner landing price vs partner payment price. Data pipeline will firstly convert them both into EUR and do the comparison. The threshold for hotel we now define price accuracy is (-5%,+5%)discrepancy percentage, based on this analysis to protect our conversion. There are three buckets of accuracy level - 
 1. Good: Price discrepancy within [-2%,+2%]discrepancy percentage. 
 2. Acceptable: Price discrepancy within [-5%,+5%]discrepancy percentage. 
 3. Bad: Price discrepancy out of (-5%,+5%)discrepancy percentage.


**Granularity**: One row per redirect(redirect id)

**Data contract**: https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AYA4Tg

**Data pipeline**: [hotel_payment_page_pa_pipeline_dag](https://skyscanner.astronomer.run/dc39jfvu/dags/beyond_flight_trusted_dag_v1/grid?orgId=org_DGv39zJ6VwGxKCw9)

**Data pipeline (Github)**: [hotelcar-data-analysis](https://github.skyscannertools.net/supply/hotelcar-data-analysis)

The package booking dataset contains booking related information for the package vertical (including revenue). This dataset can be used to find out the revenue that would be produced out of the various source systems such as partner scrapers and DV. It would contain records since the first date that the package booking data has been scraped from the partners and would be used to feed package booking data to the Daily KPI reports in the near future. The data would be updated for 365 days in this dataset during the daily runs so that any change in the booking information for the past 365 days can be captured accurately. The records would also be de-duplicated with the priority order being given to partner scrapers followed by downstream visibility for pBook data

**Granularity**: One booking per row

Country names normalized inline from the free-text country field (lowercase ISO, uppercase ISO, and full-name aliases) for the top-20 supported countries. This is a silver dataset with Skyscanner and global air traffic data. Representing potentially how much RPK (Revenue Passenger Kilometers) and number of passengers travelled is facilitated by Skyscanner. The data is derived/calculated based on silver origin destination air travel market and silver skyscanner air travel market. It includes information such as the year and month of departure, origin and destination country codes, type of haul, and both origin and destination country names. It also provides insights into the market and market group of both origin and destination countries, as well as the territory. This table can be used for understanding trends in air travel, analyzing market performance, and studying passenger traffic patterns. Note: In this dataset, we are currently using the first data available per partition on silver skyscanner air travel market.

**Granularity**: One row per origin country, destination country, departure year & departure month

**Data contract**: [Silver+-+skyscanner_share_air_travel_market](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/DATASET/pages/737088678/Silver+-+skyscanner+share+air+travel+market)

Silver layer table containing one row per itinerary shown in a flight search result session. Provides cleaned, conformed, entity-centric data from the meta search pricing pipeline for downstream analytics and reporting. Grain: one row per (search_request_id + COALESCE(itinerary_token, itinerary_id)). itinerary_token is the primary bronze source field (from Oct 2025); itinerary_id is the pre-Oct 2025 fallback. Owner: Yellow team. SLA: data available before 04:00 UTC daily. Source: prod_trusted_bronze.internal.flights_pricing_liveupdatescompleted.

Silver layer table containing one row per segment within each flight leg for every itinerary in a live-update pricing event. Provides the authoritative segment-grain source for locations, timestamps, carrier identities, flight numbers, and self-transfer flags. Grain: one row per (flight_search_result_itinerary_id + leg_number + segment_number). Owner: Yellow team. SLA: data available before 04:00 UTC daily. Source: prod_trusted_bronze.internal.flights_pricing_liveupdatescompleted.

Silver layer table capturing the full set of pricing options for each flight itinerary during a search session. One row per booking item per pricing option per itinerary per search. Mashup options are exploded to separate rows. Includes partner identity, normalized price amounts (original currency + GBP), airline fare details, and user context. Powers price volatility analysis, provider performance metrics, and conversion optimisation. Grain: one row per (flight_search_result_itinerary_id + pricing_option_position + pricing_option_item_position). Owner: Yellow team. SLA: data available before 04:00 UTC daily. Source: prod_trusted_bronze.internal.flights_pricing_liveupdatescompleted.

In downstream price accuracy, we are comparing redirect prices we show on the booking panel and the ones that partners show on their website. We use price and currency info in the redirect event and DV car hire partners events to compare both prices. Data pipeline will firstly convert them both into EUR and do the comparison. The threshold for hotel we now define price accuracy is (-5%,+5%)discrepancy percentage, based on this analysis to protect our conversion. There are three buckets of accuracy level - 
 1. Good: Price discrepancy within [-2%,+2%]discrepancy percentage. 
 2. Acceptable: Price discrepancy within [-5%,+5%]discrepancy percentage. 
 3. Bad: Price discrepancy out of (-5%,+5%)discrepancy percentage.

In downstream price accuracy, we are comparing redirect prices we show on the booking panel and the ones that partners show on their website. We use price and currency info in the redirect event and DV events to compare both prices. Data pipeline will firstly convert them both into EUR and do the comparison. The threshold for flight we now define price accuracy is (-25,+7)EUR or (-4%, 1%)discrepancy percentage, based on this analysis to protect our conversion. There are three buckets of accuracy level - 
 1. Good: Price discrepancy within (-1%,+1%)discrepancy percentage. 
 2. Acceptable: Price discrepancy within (-25,+7)EUR and (-4%, 1%)discrepancy percentage. 
 3. Bad: Price discrepancy out of (-25,+7)EUR or (-4%, 1%)discrepancy percentage.

In downstream price accuracy for both SAT and DV, we are comparing redirect prices we show on the booking panel and the ones that partners show on their website. We use price and currency info in the redirect event and SAT/DV events to compare both prices. For the same downstream events from SAT and DV which have the same redirect_id, we only remain the events from DV with higher priority. Data pipeline will firstly convert them both into EUR and do the comparison. The threshold for flight we now define price accuracy is (-25,+7)EUR or (-4%, 1%)discrepancy percentage, based on this analysis to protect our conversion. There are three buckets of accuracy level - 
 1. Good: Price discrepancy within (-1%,+1%)discrepancy percentage. 
 2. Acceptable: Price discrepancy within (-25,+7)EUR and (-4%, 1%)discrepancy percentage. 
 3. Bad: Price discrepancy out of (-25,+7)EUR or (-4%, 1%)discrepancy percentage.

After a user performs a search for flights, hotels, or car rentals, they are presented with options that match their search criteria. 
If they choose a specific option, they are taken to the Booking Panel, which shows quotes from different suppliers. 
If they then select a quote, they will be redirected to a partner website (or the Skyscanner booking platform, if booking through Skyscanner) to continue with the booking process. 
This type of event is known as a redirect or click. 
This carhire redirects dataset represents all redirects that occur across carhire verticals and includes - 
 1. Core information that is available for every redirect. Example - redirect_id, event_id etc. 
 2. Dataset has been enriched with User Agent fields. Example - os_name etc. 
 3. Estimated revenue generated per redirect. 
 4. Carhire specific information fields. Example - drop_off_location, pick_up_location etc,.

**Granularity**: One row per redirect(redirect id), no duplicate redirect id for last 183 days.

**Data contract**: [Silver+-+car+hire+redirect](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/hw2CJg)

**Data pipeline**: [silver_redirects_dag_v1](https://skyscanner.astronomer.run/d46zwutk/dags/silver_redirects_dag_v1/grid)

**Data pipeline (Github)**: [silver-redirects](https://github.com/skyscanner/silver-redirects)

After a user performs a search for flights, hotels, or car rentals, they are presented with options that match their search criteria. If they choose a specific option, they are taken to the Booking Panel, which shows quotes from different suppliers. If they then select a quote, they will be redirected to a partner website (or the Skyscanner booking platform, if booking through Skyscanner) to continue with the booking process. This type of event is known as a redirect or click. 
This flight leg dataset represents flight leg level details of all redirects that occur across flights verticals and includes - 
 1. Redirect information i.e., redirect_id 
 2. Flight leg specific information. Example - leg number, origin_routenode_id and destination_routenode_id at each leg of the flight itinerary. 
A flight leg involves a journey from an origin to a destination airport, and it may or may not include stops. If there are intermediate stops, then a flight leg is composed of two or more segments depending on the number of stops. Segment is a continuous journey from one origin airport to a destination airport with same flight_number, same aircraft and no stops in between.

**Granularity**: One row per redirect(redirect id) & leg_number, no duplicate redirect id for last 183 days.

**Data contract**: [Silver+-+flight+leg+redirect](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/yoM4KQ)

**Data pipeline**: [silver_redirects_dag_v1](https://skyscanner.astronomer.run/d46zwutk/dags/silver_redirects_dag_v1/grid)

After a user performs a search for flights, hotels, or car rentals, they are presented with options that match their search criteria. 
If they choose a specific option, they are taken to the Booking Panel, which shows quotes from different suppliers. 
If they then select a quote, they will be redirected to a partner website (or the Skyscanner booking platform, if booking through Skyscanner) to continue with the booking process. 
This type of event is known as a redirect or click. 
This redirects dataset represents all redirects that occur across flights, hotels and car-hire verticals and included core information that is available for every redirect. The dataset has been enriched with User Agent fields and estimated revenue calculated per redirect.

**Granularity**: One row per redirect(redirect id), no duplicate redirect id for last 183 days.

A search result consists of a group of itineraries generated from a users search. Each itinerary is broken down into various pricing options displayed on the booking panel page. This dataset represents the final set of pricing options shown to the user before they get redirected to the partner website. Includes TCS (Total Cost of Search) fare attributes: cabin and checked baggage details (assessment types, pieces, fees in GBP, weights in kg, inclusion flags), fare brand name, seat pre-reservation assessment, advance change assessment, and cancellation assessment. Also includes decimal-precision price columns (total_price_local, total_price_gbp) alongside legacy integer price columns.

**Granularity**: One row per search_request_id, utid, pricing_option_id, itinerary_guid, search_results_option_guid, booking_panel_id, booking_panel_option_guid, is_mashup

**Data contract**: [flight_itinerary_pricing_option](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AYCYSQ)

## app home   
  
A collection of components that are used in the app home screen.  
  
[App Home](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/Ba2xTw)  
[Recent Search](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/gXvEUw)  
This view contains the following components:
  
### App:  
**app_home_recent_search**: Recent search component on the app home screen,  
**app_home_recent_search_show_less**: Show less button in the recent search component to expand the list of recent searches,  
**app_home_recent_search_show_more**: Show more button in the recent search component to open the list of recent searches,  
**app_home_recent_search_view_group**: Recent searches are rendered (the module is only displayed when there are recent searches to populate it),  
**app_home_graphic_promo**: Graphic promo component on the app home screen,  
**app_home_homepage_hero_tapped**: Event triggered when the homepage hero ad is tapped,  
**app_home_homepage_hero_viewed**: Event triggered when the homepage hero ad is viewed,  
**app_home_nav_card**: Navigation card component on the app home screen, used to navigate to different sections of the app,

## ask skyscanner   
  
Components used in the Ask Skyscanner assistant, which provides users with an AI-powered chat interface to ask questions and receive travel-related recommendations and information.  
  
[App Docs](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/SQEyUg)  
This view contains the following components:
  
### App:  
**ask_skyscanner_assistant_loading**: Loading bubble shown when the assistant is processing a request,  
**ask_skyscanner_assistant_message**: A message from the assistant, which can be a response to a user query or an informational message,  
**ask_skyscanner_error**: A message shown when an error occurs in the assistant, including a retry button,  
**ask_skyscanner_recommendation**: A message from the assistant that includes a recommendation, such as a flight or hotel suggestion,  
**ask_skyscanner_user_message**: A message sent by the user to the assistant, typically a question or request,  
**ask_skyscanner_user_message_send**: Button to send a user message to the assistant,

**hotels_filter_meal_plan**: Filter option in the filter panel, allowing users to filter hotels by meal plan options like breakfast included,

**hotels_filter_property_type**: Filter option in the filter panel, allowing users to filter hotels by property type such as hotel, apartment, hostel etc,  
**hotels_filter_star_rating**: Filter option in the filter panel, allowing users to filter hotels by star rating,

## seo common   
  
A collection of components that are common across multiple different types of SEO pages on Skyscanner.  
  
[Popular](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/TodVQ)  
[Image Snippet](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/nAkMT)  
This view contains the following components:
  
### Web:  
**internal_links**: A tabbed, scrollable link module used to surface groups of internal SEO pages across multiple travel verticals. Links may point to city, country, route, or other pages for flights, hotels, or car hire,  
**internal_links_tab**: An individual tab in the internal links module that contains a group of links to internal SEO pages,  
**internal_links_tab_block_link**: An individual link in the internal links module that points to an internal SEO page,  
**seo_app_download_banner**: Large banner shown on an SEO page that encourages users to download the Skyscanner app,  
**seo_breadcrumbs**: A set of links at the top of an SEO page that allow users to navigate back to more general pages,  
**seo_breadcrumbs_item**: An individual link at the top of an SEO page that allows users to navigate back to a more general page,  
**seo_confidence_messaging**: Set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_item**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_item_link_0**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_item_link_1**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_item_link_2**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_v2**: Set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_v2_item**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_v2_item_link_0**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_v2_item_link_1**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_confidence_messaging_v2_item_link_2**: An individual message in the set of short messages that explain the benefits of using Skyscanner to search, compare and book,  
**seo_content_block**: Informative section that helps users get the most out of their search by offering useful tips, explanations, or booking advice.,  
**seo_content_block_item**: An individual link within the content block that links to external content for more information,  
**seo_destination_information**: Quick facts about a destination to help travellers prepare, including local rules, language, currency, and travel tips.,  
**seo_dynamic_map**: Interactive map that allows users to explore locations related to the content of the SEO page,  
**seo_dynamic_map_cta**: Explore map button that opens the interactive map on the SEO page,  
**seo_dynamic_map_item**: Show all locations button below the dynamic map,  
**seo_dynamic_map_location_item**: Individual location links displayed after opening the show all locations button on the dynamic map,  
**seo_dynamic_map_marker**: Marker displayed on the map that shows a specific location related to the content of the SEO page,  
**seo_dynamic_map_popup**: Pop up information about the location when a marker is clicked on the dynamic map,  
**seo_dynamic_map_popup_close**: Close button on the pop up information about the location when a marker is clicked on the dynamic map,  
**seo_dynamic_map_zoom_in**: Zoom in button on the dynamic map to allow users to see more detail,  
**seo_dynamic_map_zoom_out**: Zoom out button on the dynamic map to allow users to see less detail,  
**seo_faqs**: Frequently Asked Questions section on an SEO page that provides answers to common queries,  
**seo_faqs_item**: An individual question and answer pair within the FAQs section on an SEO page,  
**seo_horizontal_navigation**: A set of tabs that lets you switch between different sections of content on the page,  
**seo_horizontal_navigation_cta**: Separate button at the end of the horizontal navigation that redirects users to a different page,  
**seo_horizontal_navigation_item**: An individual tab within the horizontal navigation that allows users to switch to a different section of content on the page,  
**seo_index_block_panel**: A visual panel that highlights key benefits of using the service, with icons and short supporting messages.,  
**seo_info_block_freeform**: A flexible text section used to give tips, insights, or deeper info related to the pages content.,  
**seo_info_block_freeform_cta**: Read more button that links to additional information or a related page from the info block freeform section,  
**seo_info_snippet**: A quick-reference summary of key stats or highlights to help users understand what is available at a glance, such as the number of providers or destinations,  
**seo_info_snippet_decoupled**: A quick-reference summary showing key insights about the destination, such as popular providers, best prices, and when to book,  
**seo_info_snippet_decoupled_item**: An individual link in the quick-reference summary that refers users to a more specific search,  
**seo_info_snippet_item**: An individual link in the quick-reference summary that refers users to a more specific search,  
**seo_map_preview**: Map preview to explore locations on the SEO page,  
**seo_map_preview_cta**: Button on the map preview to open the full map to explore locations on the SEO page,  
**seo_no_deals**: Card in the deals section on an SEO page that informs users that there are no deals available for the given criteria,  
**seo_no_deals_cta**: Button on the no deals card that redirects users to perform another search,  
**seo_popular_destinations**: Popular destinations section on the SEO pages that highlights popular destinations,  
**seo_popular_destinations_item**: Individual popular destination card shown in the popular destinations section on the SEO pages,  
**seo_popular_destinations_next**: Next button for the popular destinations section to view more popular destinations on the SEO pages,  
**seo_popular_destinations_prev**: Previous button for the popular destinations section to view previous popular destinations on the SEO pages,  
**seo_provider_logos**: A collection of provider logos displayed on the SEO pages showing providers available,  
**seo_provider_logos_item**: An individual logo of a provider available displayed on the SEO pages,  
**seo_provider_rating**: Rating section on the SEO pages that shows the average ratings of a provider based on user reviews,  
**seo_provider_rating_big**: Rating section on the SEO pages that shows the average ratings of a provider based on user reviews,  
**seo_provider_rating_item**: An individual rating item shown in the provider rating section on the SEO pages,  
**seo_provider_rating_next**: Next button for the provider rating section to view more ratings on the SEO pages,  
**seo_provider_rating_prev**: Previous button for the provider rating section to view previous ratings on the SEO pages,  
**seo_redirect_link_banner**: Banner shown on an SEO page that encourages users to click through to a specific search result,  
**seo_redirect_link_banner_cta**: Button on the redirect link banner that redirects users to a specific search result page,  
**seo_seo_cross_links_v2**: Section on an SEO page that contains links to other vertical home pages,  
**seo_seo_cross_links_v2_item**: An individual link in the section on an SEO page that contains links to other vertical home page,  
**seo_seo_ums_cross_links_v2**: Section on an SEO page that contains links to other vertical pages or searches,  
**seo_seo_ums_cross_links_v2_item**: An individual link in the section on an SEO page that contains links to other vertical pages or searches,  
**seo_trustpilot_reviews**: Section on an SEO page that displays Skyscanners Trustpilot review score,  
**seo_trustpilot_reviews_link**: Link to Skyscanners Trustpilot page to read more reviews,  
**seo_best_time_to_visit_month_picker_item**: Link showing cheapest flight price for a month on the best time to visit section card in an SEO page,  
**seo_image_snippet_list**: A group of image snippets that provide visual content related to the SEO page, for example car options,  
**seo_image_snippet_list_next**: Next button for the image snippet list to view more images related to the SEO page,  
**seo_image_snippet_list_prev**: Previous button for the image snippet list to view previous images related to the SEO page,

**seo_provider_logos_item**: An individual logo of a provider available displayed on the SEO pages,  
**seo_provider_rating**: Rating section on the SEO pages that shows the average ratings of a provider based on user reviews,  
**seo_provider_rating_big**: Rating section on the SEO pages that shows the average ratings of a provider based on user reviews,  
**seo_provider_rating_item**: An individual rating item shown in the provider rating section on the SEO pages,  
**seo_provider_rating_next**: Next button for the provider rating section to view more ratings on the SEO pages,  
**seo_provider_rating_prev**: Previous button for the provider rating section to view previous ratings on the SEO pages,

**seo_redirect_link_banner_cta**: Button on the redirect link banner that redirects users to a specific search result page,

**seo_seo_cross_links_v2_item**: An individual link in the section on an SEO page that contains links to other vertical home page,  
**seo_seo_ums_cross_links_v2**: Section on an SEO page that contains links to other vertical pages or searches,  
**seo_seo_ums_cross_links_v2_item**: An individual link in the section on an SEO page that contains links to other vertical pages or searches,

**seo_trustpilot_reviews_link**: Link to Skyscanners Trustpilot page to read more reviews,
